calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2022 Continued To Aug-17-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-05-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendants Long Nguyen and LIN Holding Company, having been continued from February 17, 2022.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:16 a.m.

Gregory S. Walston, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Elmer Li, whose appearance for today's hearing was waived at the last calling of the case on Februray 17, 2022.
Mr. Walston also appeared for plaintiff Mi Young Li, who was not present.
Mr. Walston appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.

Plaintiffs' Complaint for Fraud, Negligent Misrepresentation, Breach of Contract, Breach of Fiduciary Duty, Breach of Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing, Unfair Business Practices, Conversion and Request for Punitive Damages was filed on February 11, 2021.
Substitute service of plaintiffs' Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendants Long Nguyen and LIN Holding Company occurred on March 15, 2021 and was effectuated on March 26, 2021.
Four separate Statement of Damages, one from each plaintiff to each defendant, were served on June 16, 2021.
Default as to each defendant was entered on June 16,m 2021.
DOE defendants were dismissed on November 24, 2022.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Elmer Li and Mi Young, and against the defaulted defendants Long Nguyen, an individual, and LIN Holding Company, a business entity, form unknown, as follows:

Fraud Damages - $275,000.00.*
Prejudgment Interest - $91,025.00.
Costs - $720.00.
Punitive Damages - $2,000,000.00.

Total - $2,366,745.00.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Proceedings concluded at 10:43 a.m.

*The Court did not award plaintiffs $585,000.00 for past contract damages, nor $7,668,000.00 for future contract damages.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Continued To May-18-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Feb-17-2022 Continued To Apr-05-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendants Long Nguyen and LIN Holding Company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:54 a.m.

Gregory S. Walston, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Elmer Li, who was placed under oath and testified.
Mr. Walston also appeared for plaintiff Mi Young Li, who was not present.
Plaintiff Elmer Li was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. Walston and Li appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.

Plaintiffs' Complaint for Fraud, Negligent Misrepresentation, Breach of Contract, Breach of Fiduciary Duty, Breach of Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing, Unfair Business Practices, Conversion and Request for Punitive Damages was filed on February 11, 2021.
Substitute service of plaintiffs' Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendants Long Nguyen and LIN Holding Company occurred on March 15, 2021 and was effectuated on March 26, 2021.
Four separate Statement of Damages, one from each plaintiff to each defendant, were served on June 16, 2021.
Default as to each defendant was entered on June 16,m 2021.
DOE defendants were dismissed on November 24, 2022.

Good cause appearing, the matter is CONTINUED to April 5,2022 for further default prove-up on the issue issue damages. Plaintiff Elmer Li's testimony has been completed and his appearance is waived at the next calling of the case. Plaintiffs shall provide defendants with notice of the continued hearing date.

Proceedings concluded at 11:26 a.m.
